hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions in off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are capable of achieving strong selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural instability and poor docking scores are limiting success. Tightening structural constraints and increasing candidate diversity should help converge on a stable, selective design.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better structural quality and reduce steric clashes that likely contribute to low pLDDT and failed docking.
Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating structurally valid and selective designs.
